message.entry: Array conversion failed.
Source and destination array dimension sizes do not match.
Source and destination array lengths do not match.
Number of dimensions in the source and destination arrays do not match.
Cell contents assignment to a non-cell array object.
Cell contents reference from a non-cell array object.
Error marshalling .NET object.\n ''{0}''
Creating an empty .NET object is not supported.
Array index must be a positive integer.
Could not find class ''{0}''.
Invalid generic parameter type argument.
No appropriate method, property, or field ''{0}'' for class ''{1}''.
Attempting to access the property or method of an invalid object.
The name ''{0}'' is not an accessible property for an instance of class ''{1}''.
Static property ''{0}'' is not accessible for class ''{1}''.
Could not find static property or field ''{0}'' for class ''{1}''.
Invalid static property name ''{0}''.
Setting the static property ''{0}'' is not allowed for an instance of class ''{1}''.
Could not find namespace ''{0}'' for the type ''{1}''.
Could not find generic method ''{0}''.
Could not find generic method ''{0}'' with matching signature.
Property or field ''{0}'' is not static.
No constructor ''{0}'' with matching signature found.
The namespace for the type ''{0}'' does not exist. MATLAB requires all .NET types to be within a namespace.
Attempt to index a ''null'' object.
Object creation failed.
Could not get package ''{0}''.

The .NET security policy setting on this machine does not allow running any MATLAB Interface to .NET commands from a network drive. Please visit the following documentation page for details. .NET Security Policy Settings
Could not load the type ''{0}''.
.NET events with nonstandard delegate definition are not supported in MATLAB.
Array formation is not allowed on .NET objects.
''end'' index is not supported for .NET objects.
Array formation and indexing are not allowed on .NET objects.
Array formation and indexing are not allowed on .NET objects. Use Set and Get methods to access .NET array elements.
Array formation and indexing are not allowed on .NET objects. Use the Item method to index into this object.
Conversion from ''{0}'' array type is not supported.
Conversion to ''{0}'' array type is not supported.
Error occurred while executing delegate callback:\n{0}
Error occurred while executing method:\n{0}
Implementation of .NET interfaces is not supported.
Implementation of .NET interfaces defining properties is not supported.
Implementation of .NET interfaces defining indexers is not supported.
Implementation of .NET interfaces defining events is not supported.
Implementation of generic .NET interfaces is not supported.
Implementation of .NET interfaces defining generic methods is not supported.
Implementation of .NET COM interfaces is not supported.
Class implementing .NET interface must be a subclass of handle.

Cannot choose between the following .NET method signatures due to unspecified optional arguments in the call to {0}\n\n You can resolve this ambiguity by specifying enough additional optional arguments so that there is only one possible matching .NET method.
No method ''{0}'' with matching signature found for class ''{1}''.
NET.Assembly handle
\n Package: NET\n
Class ''{0}'' declares the .NET class ''{1}'' as a superclass. Creating a MATLAB subclass of a .NET class is not supported.
.NET commands require Framework 4.0 or higher. Please install Framework version 4.0 or higher before running any .NET commands.\n
Invalid optional parameter name {0} for the method 'cell'. The parameter available is 'ConvertTypes'.
Invalid .NET type {0} specified for conversion. The value should be a cell array containing ''all'' or the following supported types, ''System.String'',''System.Datetime'.
Expecting cell array of .NET types to be converted to MATLAB types.
No method 'cell' with matching signature found for class 'System.Object[,]'.
"Unknown error."
Message: {0}\nSource: {1}\nHelpLink: {2}
"Unknown method invocation error."
"Unknown delegate invocation error."
Unable to load .NET object. Saving (serializing) .NET objects into a MAT-file is not supported.
